q.en: Where and when was Ibn Battuta born?
q.zh: 伊本·白图泰出生在何处、何时？

options.en: In 1304 in Tangier, Morocco
options.zh: 1304 年，摩洛哥丹吉尔

options.en: In 1325 in Mecca
In 1368 in Delhi, India
options.zh: 1368 年，印度德里

options.en: In 1250 in Baghdad
options.zh: 1250 年，巴格达

explain.en: The book states: 'He was born in 1304 in Tangier, Morocco. Continuing a family tradition, he studied law.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“他于 1304 年出生在摩洛哥的丹吉尔。遵循家族传统，他学习了法律。”

q.en: In the glossary, what does 'unpredictable' mean?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，“unpredictable”是什么意思？

options.en: Impossible to know ahead of time; able to change without warning
options.zh: 无法提前预知；可能毫无征兆地发生变化

options.en: A person who visits and learns about new places
options.zh: 参观并了解新地方的人

options.en: People who aggressively enter a place to conquer it
options.zh: 闯入某地意图征服的人

options.en: A type of fabric made from silkworm threads
options.zh: 由蚕丝制成的一种织物

explain.en: The Glossary defines unpredictable as 'impossible to know ahead of time; able to change without warning (p. 7),' used to describe the sultan of India.
explain.zh: 词汇表将 unpredictable 定义为“无法提前预知；可能毫无征兆地发生变化（第 7 页）”，用来形容印度的苏丹。

q.en: Why did Ibn Battuta first set out for Mecca in 1325?
q.zh: 伊本·白图泰在 1325 年最初动身前往麦加的原因是什么？

options.en: Muslims have a duty to travel to Mecca at least once if they can
options.zh: 伊斯兰教徒若力所能及，有职责至少前往麦加一次

options.en: To become a judge in Delhi
options.zh: 为了在德里担任法官

options.en: To escape a shipwreck in China
options.zh: 为了躲避在中国的海难

options.en: To visit the sultan of Mali
options.zh: 为了拜访马里苏丹

explain.en: The book says Mecca is where Islam was founded and 'Muslims have a duty to travel to Mecca at least once if they can,' which drew the young student there in 1325.
explain.zh: 书中说麦加是伊斯兰教发源地，“伊斯兰教徒若力所能及，有职责至少前往麦加一次”，这吸引这位年轻学子于 1325 年前往。

q.en: How did Ibn Battuta usually travel for safety?
q.zh: 为了安全，伊本·白图泰通常如何旅行？

options.en: Mostly by camel with large groups, traveling at night with torches
options.zh: 主要马马马马、与大队伍同行，夜间举火把赶路

options.en: Alone by boat across the Indian Ocean
options.zh: 独自乘船横渡印度洊

options.en: By airplane with the emperor
options.zh: 与皇帝一起乘飞机

options.en: On foot through the desert by himself
options.zh: 独自步行穿越沙漠

explain.en: The book says: 'Ibn Battuta traveled mostly by camel with large groups. Traveling with many other people provided safety from bandits... traveled in the cool of the night, lighting the way with torches.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“伊本·白图泰主要马马马、与大队伍同行。与众多人同行可免受强盗侵害……他们在凉爽的夜间赶路，用火把照明。”

q.en: According to the book, how did the Muslim faith help Ibn Battuta during his journeys?
q.zh: 根据本书，伊斯兰教信在旅途中如何帮助了伊本·白图泰？

options.en: It inspired people to give money and gifts to travelers, so he received many gifts and honors
options.zh: 它促使人们向旅行者赠送金钱和礼物，因此他收到了许多馈赏与礼遇

options.en: It paid for his ships to China
options.zh: 它为他前往中国的船只付了钱

options.en: It protected him from the plague in Damascus
options.zh: 它使他在大马士革免遭瘙疫

options.en: It taught him how to be a judge
options.zh: 它教会了他如何当法官

explain.en: The book states: 'The Muslim faith inspired many people to give money and gifts to travelers. Because Ibn Battuta was a student and eventually a famed traveler, he received many gifts and honors.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“伊斯兰教信促使许多人向旅行者赠送金钱和礼物。由于伊本·白图泰是学生并最终成为著名旅行家，他收到了许多馈赏与礼遇。”

q.en: What happened to Ibn Battuta on his way to China?
q.zh: 伊本·白图泰在前往中国的途中发生了什么？

options.en: His group was attacked by rebels, their ships were destroyed in a storm, and treasure was lost
options.zh: 他的队伍遭叛军袭击，船只被风暴摧骤，财宝尽失

options.en: He won a war against the sultan of India
options.zh: 他打赢了对印度苏丹的战争

options.en: He married the emperor of Constantinople
options.zh: 他与君士坦丁堡的皇帝结婚

options.en: He caught the plague in Cairo
options.zh: 他在开罗染上瘙疫

explain.en: The book reports: 'Ibn Battuta's group was attacked by rebels and had to split up. Later, the ships... were destroyed by a big storm. All the treasure aboard the ships was lost.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“伊本·白图泰的队伍遭叛军袭击，被迫分散。后来，他们本要乘坐的船只被一场大风暴摧骤，船上所有财宝都损失了。”

q.en: In India, what position did the sultan of Delhi give Ibn Battuta?
q.zh: 在印度，德里苏丹给了伊本·白图泰什么职位？

options.en: He made him a judge
options.zh: 任命他为法官

options.en: He made him a general
options.zh: 任命他为将军

options.en: He made him a ship captain
options.zh: 任命他为船长

options.en: He made him a farmer
options.zh: 让他务农

explain.en: Under 'In Trouble in India' the book says: 'The sultan lived up to his reputation for generosity. He gave Ibn Battuta many gifts and later made him a judge.'
explain.zh: 在“在印度的困境”一节中，书中说：“苏丹果然以愧憤著称，他送给伊本·白图泰许多礼物，后来还任命他为法官。”

options.en: People who aggressively enter a place to conquer, weaken, or injure its people
options.zh: aggressively 闯入某地、意图征服、剰弱或伤害其人民的人

options.en: People who journey from one place to another
options.zh: 从一个地方到另一个地方旅行的人

options.en: People who speak or act out against authority
options.zh: 以言行反抗权威的人

options.en: A person who betrays his own country
options.zh: 背叛自己国家的人

explain.en: The Glossary defines invaders as 'people who aggressively enter a place to conquer, weaken, or injure its people (p. 7),' noting Iraq and Iran were recovering from attacks by invaders.
explain.zh: 词汇表将 invaders 定义为“aggressively 闯入某地、意图征服、剰弱或伤害其人民的人（第 7 页）”，并提到伊拉克和伊朗当时正从入侵者的攻击中恢复。

q.en: The book says Ibn Battuta was 'not an explorer' because he visited no unknown lands. Why does it still call him one of the greatest travelers in history?
q.zh: 书中说伊本·白图泰“不是探险家”，因为他没有到访过未知之地。为何仍称他为史上最伟大的旅行家之一？

options.en: He journeyed 75,000 miles (like going around the world three times) and visited dozens of countries
options.zh: 他旅行了 7.5 万英里（相当于绕地球三圈），到访了数十个国家

options.en: He discovered a new continent
options.zh: 他发现了一块新大陆

options.en: He wrote the Muslim holy book
options.zh: 他撰写了伊斯兰教圣书

options.en: He ruled the Mali Empire
options.zh: 他统治了马里帝国

explain.en: The Conclusion states: 'Ibn Battuta journeyed 75,000 miles (120,000 km) during his lifetime. That's like going all the way around the world three times! ... Nevertheless, he remains one of the greatest travelers in history.'
explain.zh: 结论写道：“伊本·白图泰一生旅行了 7.5 万英里（12 万公里）。这相当于绕地球三圈！……尽管如此，他仍是史上最伟大的旅行家之一。”

q.en: How long was Ibn Battuta away from home on his first great journey, and how far did he travel in his lifetime?
q.zh: 伊本·白图泰首次伟大旅程离家多久？他一生旅行了多远？

options.en: He was gone 24 years, and traveled 75,000 miles in his lifetime
options.zh: 他离家 24 年，一生旅行了 7.5 万英里

options.en: He was gone 8 years, and traveled 3,000 miles
options.zh: 他离家 8 年，旅行了 3 千英里

options.en: He was gone 1 year, and traveled 1,000 miles
options.zh: 他离家 1 年，旅行了 1 千英里

options.en: He was gone 50 years, and traveled 120 miles
options.zh: 他离家 50 年，旅行了 120 英里

explain.en: The book says he returned to Morocco in 1349 after being 'gone for twenty-four years,' and the Conclusion notes he journeyed '75,000 miles (120,000 km) during his lifetime.'
explain.zh: 书中说他于 1349 年回到摩洛哥，此前已“离家二十四年”；结论提到他一生旅行了“7.5 万英里（12 万公里）”。

prompt.en: Pretend you are Ibn Battuta. Write a postcard home to your family describing your experiences at one of the places you visited (such as Mecca, Delhi, the Maldive Islands, or China).
prompt.zh: 假设你就是伊本·白图泰。写一张寄给家人的明信片，描述你到访某地（如麦加、德里、马尔代夫群岛或中国）的经历。

en: Use a real place and a fact from the book (e.g., silk worn even by beggars in China, or the sultan making him a judge in Delhi).
Write from first person and include what you saw, whom you met, and how you felt.
zh: 使用书中真实地点和事实（例如中国连与死都穿丝绸，或德里苏丹任命他为法官）。
用第一人称写，包含你看到的景象、遇到的人以及你的感受。
